title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
summary:
Between life and death there is a library, and within that library, the shelves go on forever. Every book provides a chance to try another life you could have lived. To see how things would be if you had made other choices... Would you have done anything different, if you had the chance to undo your regrets?

Nora Seed finds herself faced with this decision. Faced with the possibility of changing her life for a new one, following a different career, undoing old breakups, realizing her dreams of becoming a glaciologist; she must search within herself as she travels through the Midnight Library to decide what is truly fulfilling in life, and what makes it worth living in the first place.

A dazzling novel about all the choices that go into a life well lived, from the internationally bestselling author of Reasons to Stay Alive and How To Stop Time.

chapters.title: Chapter 1: The Midnight Library
chapters.content:
The fog rolled off the harbor in thick, grey sheets, swallowing the gaslamps one by one. Elara pulled her coat tighter, the wool coarse against her chin. The scent of salt and decaying wood hung heavy in the air, a familiar perfume to anyone born on the docks. She walked briskly, her boots making no sound on the damp cobblestones, a trick she'd learned from years of necessity.

Ahead, the silhouette of the old custom house loomed like a dormant beast. It was here she was supposed to meet him. 'Midnight,' the note had read, written in that sprawling, elegant hand she hadn't seen in nearly a decade. She checked her pocket watch by the dim light of a surviving lamp; five minutes to spare. The silence of the city was profound, broken only by the distant, mournful groan of a ship's horn.

She reached the heavy iron gates and pushed. They yielded with a rusted shriek that set her teeth on edge. Inside, the courtyard was a tangle of overgrown weeds and discarded crates. She stepped carefully, her eyes adjusting to the deeper gloom.

"You're late," a voice whispered from the shadows near the entrance to the main hall. It was soft, almost carried away by the damp wind, but it stopped her cold.

Elara turned slowly. "I'm exactly on time, Silas. It's you who is hiding." He stepped out from behind a cracked stone pillar, the collar of his trench coat turned up against the chill. He looked older, the lines around his eyes deeper, but the sharp intelligence in his gaze remained undimmed.

"Prudence, Elara. The city isn't as asleep as it seems," he murmured, gesturing for her to follow him inside. The interior smelled of old paper and dust. They navigated through a labyrinth of forgotten desks and filing cabinets until they reached a small office in the back. A single candle flickered on the desk, illuminating a large, rolled map.

He didn't waste time on pleasantries. "They've moved it," he said, tapping a finger on a specific location on the map. "The artifact isn't in the cathedral crypt anymore. It's beneath the university library." Elara sighed, a long, weary sound. "Of course it is. Why would anything be simple?"

Silas offered a grim smile. "Simple is for people who don't know the value of what they're looking for. We go in tomorrow night. Are you ready?" Elara looked at the map, tracing the lines of the city she knew so well, a city that held secrets darker than the fog outside. She nodded once. "I'm ready."

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
summary: Ryland Grace is the sole survivor on a desperate, last-chance mission—and if he fails, humanity and the earth itself will perish. Except that right now, he doesn't know that. He can't even remember his own name, let alone the nature of his assignment or how to complete it.

chapters.title: Chapter 1: Waking Up
chapters.content:
I open my eyes. The room is stark white. Fluorescent lights hum overhead with a clinical buzz that makes my teeth ache. I'm lying on something soft — a bed, I think. My body feels wrong. Heavy, like I've been asleep for years. Which, as it turns out, is exactly the case.

I try to sit up but my muscles protest with a scream of atrophied fury. A mechanical arm swings over from somewhere above, offering me a tube of something. Food? Medicine? I honestly can't tell. My brain feels like it's running on a processor from the 1990s — every thought takes an eternity to form.

Where am I? Who am I? The questions cascade, each one triggering a small avalanche of panic. I look around for clues. The walls are covered in screens, all dark. There are two other beds in the room. The shapes in them are... still. Too still. A cold knot forms in my stomach.

I manage to swing my legs off the edge of the bed. The floor is cold against my bare feet. I take a tentative step, then another. The room sways, or maybe I do. I grab the railing of the bed for support and wait for the world to stabilize.

A panel on the far wall lights up. It displays a series of numbers and a single word: GRACE. Is that my name? It doesn't feel like anything. It's just a word. But it's the only clue I have, so I'll take it. My name is Grace. And I need to figure out what the hell is going on.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
summary: Set on the desert planet Arrakis, Dune is the story of the boy Paul Atreides, heir to a noble family tasked with ruling an inhospitable world where the only thing of value is the 'spice' melange, a drug capable of extending life and enhancing consciousness.

chapters.title: Chapter 1: Arrakis
chapters.content:
In the week before their departure to Arrakis, when all the final hopes of opposition had died, the Lady Jessica permitted herself a moment of grief. She stood at the window of her morning room in Castle Caladan, looking out over the orchards.

The sun was warm on her face, a golden warmth she knew she would not feel again for a very long time. Arrakis. The desert planet. A world of sand and wind and the most valuable substance in the universe: the spice melange.

Her son, Paul, was somewhere in the training halls below, learning the ways of combat from Gurney Halleck, or perhaps studying the Mentat disciplines under Thufir Hawat. At fifteen, he was already more capable than most men twice his age. But he was still a boy, and she was still a mother, and she worried.

The Duke had told her his reasons for accepting the fief of Arrakis. It was a trap, of course. Everyone knew it was a trap. The Harkonnens had held Arrakis for eighty years, growing fat on spice revenues, and they would not give it up willingly. But the Emperor had commanded, and Leto Atreides was, above all things, a man of duty.

She heard footsteps behind her — Paul's light, careful tread. "Mother?" She turned from the window, composing her features. "Yes, Paul." He looked at her with those old-young eyes. "I've been thinking about Arrakis," he said.

title: Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow
author: Gabrielle Zevin
summary: Sam Masur and Sadie Green meet as kids in a hospital gaming room. Years later, they reconnect in a crowded train station and decide to create a video game together, a decision that will change both of their lives.

chapters.title: Chapter 1: Sick Kids
chapters.content:
Before Mazer came to the hospital, Sam had been playing Super Mario Bros. alone. He played every day, from the moment he was wheeled into the common room after his physical therapy until the nurses came to take him back. The game was his escape, his portal to a world where broken bodies didn't exist.

He was stuck on World 8-3 when she appeared. A girl, roughly his age, with dark, serious eyes and a mass of curly brown hair that seemed to defy the laws of hospital gravity. She watched him play for several minutes without saying a word.

"You need to run under the Hammer Bros," she said finally. "Don't try to jump over them. You'll never make it." Sam looked at her. "I know what I'm doing," he said, even though he clearly didn't. He died again, and the girl sighed — a world-weary sound for someone so young.

"Can I try?" she asked. He handed over the controller reluctantly. She cleared the level on her first attempt. Sam stared at the screen, then at her. "I'm Sadie," she said, handing back the controller with a small, triumphant smile. "I'm Sam." And that was how it began.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
summary: An Easy & Proven Way to Build Good Habits & Break Bad Ones. No matter your goals, Atomic Habits offers a proven framework for improving — every day.

chapters.title: Chapter 1: The Surprising Power of Atomic Habits
chapters.content:
It is so easy to overestimate the importance of one defining moment and underestimate the value of making small improvements on a daily basis. Too often, we convince ourselves that massive success requires massive action.

Meanwhile, improving by 1 percent isn't particularly notable — sometimes it isn't even noticeable — but it can be far more meaningful, especially in the long run. The difference a tiny improvement can make over time is astounding. Here's how the math works out: if you can get 1 percent better each day for one year, you'll end up thirty-seven times better by the time you're done.

Habits are the compound interest of self-improvement. The same way that money multiplies through compound interest, the effects of your habits multiply as you repeat them. They seem to make little difference on any given day and yet the impact they deliver over the months and years can be enormous. It is only when looking back two, five, or perhaps ten years later that the value of good habits and the cost of bad ones becomes strikingly apparent.

This can be a difficult concept to appreciate in daily life. We often dismiss small changes because they don't seem to matter very much in the moment. If you save a little money now, you're still not a millionaire. If you go to the gym three days in a row, you're still out of shape. If you study Mandarin for an hour tonight, you still haven't learned the language.

title: Normal People
author: Sally Rooney
summary: Connell and Marianne grow up in the same small town in the west of Ireland, but the similarities end there. In school, Connell is popular. Marianne is not. A year later, at Trinity College Dublin, Marianne is popular. Connell is not.

chapters.title: Chapter 1: January 2011
chapters.content:
Marianne answers the door when Connell rings the bell. She's still in her school uniform, and she doesn't smile. She just says: Oh. Hi. Come in. She turns and walks down the hallway and he follows her.

His mother Lorraine works here in Marianne's house. She cleans it. It's a big house on the hill, with a gravel driveway and two stone pillars at the gate. Connell has been here before, but not recently. He finds the interior of the house somehow gloomy and cold, with high ceilings and big furniture.

Marianne leads him through the hall and into the kitchen. The kitchen is very large and has French doors opening onto the garden. Lorraine is mopping the floor. She leans the mop against the counter when she sees Connell. Hiya, she says. How was the match? Good, yeah, he says. We won. Lorraine smiles and touches his arm. That's great, love.

Marianne sits at the kitchen table and opens her laptop. She's looking at something on the screen. Connell stands near the door, uncertain of where to put himself. He feels oversized in this house, with its delicate ornaments and framed art prints. His hands seem too big, his shoes too dirty.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
summary: Alicia Berenson's life is seemingly perfect. Until one evening, when she shoots her husband five times in the face. And she never speaks another word.

chapters.title: Chapter 1: Alicia
chapters.content:
Alicia Berenson was thirty-three years old when she killed her husband. She shot Gabriel in the face five times. Then she never spoke another word.

The police found her standing in the hallway, the gun dangling from her right hand, her white dress splattered with blood. Gabriel was in the kitchen, slumped over the table. His face was unrecognizable. The kitchen was a mess of blood and broken china.

Alicia was taken into custody. She was questioned for hours but said nothing. Not a single word. The police, the lawyers, the psychiatrists — they all tried. She remained silent. It was as if the act of killing had silenced something inside her, had flipped a switch that could not be flipped back.

The media went wild, of course. 'THE SILENT WIFE,' the tabloids screamed. 'BEAUTY KILLS THE BEAST.' Alicia had been a successful painter, quite well known in certain circles, and the story of a beautiful artist who murders her photographer husband had all the ingredients the public craved: glamour, mystery, violence.

But the silence was what fascinated people the most. Why wouldn't she speak? What was she hiding? What terrible truth was locked behind those sealed lips?

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
summary: An unforgettable memoir about a young girl who, kept out of school, leaves her survivalist family and goes on to earn a PhD from Cambridge University.

chapters.title: Chapter 1: Buck's Peak
chapters.content:
I'm standing on the red railway car that sits on a hill at the base of Buck's Peak. The hill is steep, and from the top of the car I can see the entire valley. I can see our house, the only one on the whole mountain. It's a squat building, so low to the ground that from a distance it looks like a pile of stones.

The mountain is beautiful in the early morning light, its peaks still dusted with last night's snow. The valley below is a patchwork of fields — some green, some brown — bisected by the old highway that runs through town. Town is generous; it's really just a scattering of buildings clustered around a gas station and a church.

My father stands in the junkyard below, a cigarette in one hand, a wrench in the other. He's sorting through a pile of scrap — old car parts, copper wiring, aluminum cans. The junkyard is his kingdom, a sprawling empire of rusted metal and broken machinery that stretches across several acres. To most people, it would look like chaos. To my father, it's a library of useful things.

"Tara!" he shouts up at me. "Get down from there! We've got work to do!" I climb down reluctantly. There's always work to do.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
summary: A Brief History of Humankind. One hundred thousand years ago, at least six different species of humans inhabited Earth. Today there is just one – us.

chapters.title: Chapter 1: An Animal of No Significance
chapters.content:
About 13.5 billion years ago, matter, energy, time and space came into being in what is known as the Big Bang. The story of these fundamental features of our universe is called physics.

About 300,000 years after their appearance, matter and energy started to coalesce into complex structures, called atoms, which then combined into molecules. The story of atoms, molecules and their interactions is called chemistry.

About 3.8 billion years ago, on a planet called Earth, certain molecules combined to form particularly large and intricate structures called organisms. The story of organisms is called biology.

About 70,000 years ago, organisms belonging to the species Homo sapiens started to form even more elaborate structures called cultures. The subsequent development of these human cultures is called history.

Three important revolutions shaped the course of history: the Cognitive Revolution kick-started history about 70,000 years ago. The Agricultural Revolution sped it up about 12,000 years ago. The Scientific Revolution, which got under way only 500 years ago, may well end history and start something completely different.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
summary: A magical story about Santiago, an Andalusian shepherd boy who yearns to travel in search of a worldly treasure as extravagant as any ever found.

chapters.title: Part One
chapters.content:
The boy's name was Santiago. Dusk was falling as the boy arrived with his herd at an abandoned church. The roof had fallen in long ago, and an enormous sycamore had grown on the spot where the sacristy had once stood.

He decided to spend the night there. He saw to it that all the sheep entered through the ruined gate, and then laid some planks across it to prevent the flock from wandering away during the night. There were no wolves in the region, but once an animal had strayed during the night, and the boy had had to spend the entire next day searching for it.

He swept the floor with his jacket and lay down, using the book he had just finished reading as a pillow. He told himself that he would have to start reading thicker books: they lasted longer, and made more comfortable pillows.

It was still dark when he woke, and looking up, he could see the stars through the half-destroyed roof. I wanted to sleep a little longer, he thought. He had had the same dream that night as a week ago, and once again he had woken before it ended.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
summary: The guru to the gurus at last offers his own book – a tour de force of the mind, revealing the two systems that drive the way we think.

chapters.title: Chapter 1: The Characters of the Story
chapters.content:
To observe your mind in automatic mode, glance at the following image. Your experience as you look at the woman's face seamlessly combines what we normally call seeing and intuitive thinking. As surely and quickly as you saw that the young woman's hair is dark, you knew that she is angry.

Furthermore, what you saw extended into the future. You sensed that this woman is about to say some very unkind words, probably in a loud and strident voice. A premonition of what she was going to do next came to mind automatically and effortlessly. You did not intend to assess her mood or to anticipate what she might do, and your reaction to the picture did not have the feel of something you did. It just happened to you. It was an instance of fast thinking.

Now look at the following problem: 17 × 24. You knew immediately that this is a multiplication problem, and probably knew that you could solve it, with paper and pencil if not without. You also had some vague intuitive knowledge of the range of possible results. You would be quick to recognize that both 12,609 and 123 are implausible.

Without spending some time on the problem, however, you would not be certain that the answer is not 568. A precise solution did not come to mind, and you felt that you could choose whether or not to engage in the computation. If you have not done so yet, you should attempt the multiplication now, completing at least part of it.

title: Where the Crawdads Sing
author: Delia Owens
summary: For years, rumors of the 'Marsh Girl' have haunted Barkley Cove, a quiet town on the North Carolina coast. So in late 1969, when handsome Chase Andrews is found dead, the locals immediately suspect Kya Clark.

chapters.title: Chapter 1: The Marsh
chapters.content:
Marsh is not swamp. Marsh is a space of light, where grass grows in water, and water flows into the sky. Slow-moving creeks wander, carrying the salted water to and from the sea, which can be heard sometimes softly slapping in the distance.

Then within the marsh, here and there, stand small islands of bushes and trees so dense they seem to float, like boats on still water. The boy ran, splashing through shallow water and sand, onto the bank and up the slope. He stopped abruptly. His eyes rounded wide.

Two boys from the village, Chase Andrews and his friend, called out to the marsh girl. They hollered and laughed as she slipped through the tall grass like a shadow. They never caught her; she knew the marsh like a muskrat knows its den.

Kya had never owned shoes, but she could feel the shells and acorns beneath her soles with a familiarity that was almost affectionate. The marsh taught her everything: how to listen, how to move, how to be still. The marsh was her mother, her father, and her only school.

She squatted behind a bush and watched the boys from a distance, her dark eyes unblinking, absorbing their carelessness and confidence — things as foreign to her as snow. When they finally gave up and walked back to the village, she waited until their voices faded, then stood and walked to the water's edge.
